content.claim: Administrative desegregation rules implemented in the 2000s produced temporary reductions in urban segregation and selection by ethnicity and social status, but these measures stagnated mid-decade and could only mitigate rather than eliminate negative trends.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Partial results reflect administrative measures applied without track abolition; cannot isolate track system's independent effect
No counterfactual scenario comparing segregation outcomes with vs. without tracks under identical administrative conditions
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
